profile.name: Akash Chauhan
profile.title: Senior Frontend Multi-Stack Developer
profile.summary: I'm a Senior Frontend Developer with over 4 years of experience building web applications that are fast, accessible, and genuinely enjoyable to use. I've had the pleasure of working across ReactJS, VueJS, and Angular, which has helped me adapt comfortably to different team environments and deliver thoughtful solutions even under tight timelines.
profile.highlights: I've had the opportunity to help shape enterprise-scale platforms, share what I've learned through technical writing and department training sessions, and put together an AI Prompt Library (v1.2) featuring 25+ ready-to-use prompts that the entire Vue.js team has found helpful in their day-to-day work.

projects.name: Internal Workflow & Productivity Platform
projects.description: I'm currently working on an internal workflow and productivity platform for our enterprise teams. It's been a great opportunity to shape the frontend architecture from the ground up, develop reusable components, and collaborate closely with colleagues across the Vue.js department. This one is still actively in progress as of February 2026.

projects.name: AI Creator & Fan Interaction Platform
projects.description: This was a really engaging project — an AI-powered platform connecting creators with their fans. I took ownership of the entire frontend, setting up Pinia for state management and building a thorough route guard system that handles authentication, verification, and subscription logic for Creator, Fan, and Admin roles. Along the way I also built out the real-time chat, integrated Stripe payments, handled OAuth sign-ins via Google and Apple, and put together a clean, accessible UI using Ant Design Vue and SCSS.

projects.name: Enterprise Real-Time Discovery Platform
projects.description: Joining this project meant getting up to speed with Angular very quickly — I was contributing meaningfully within two weeks of onboarding, which I'm genuinely proud of. I implemented SignalR to support real-time notifications and broadcasts, built a flexible global drawer service, and wired up Apache ECharts to bring complex data to life visually.

projects.name: Healthcare Management Platform
projects.description: I stepped into a legacy codebase here, which required patience and careful debugging — especially around the Cloudflare integration. I made sure to leave things better than I found them, writing detailed handover documentation that covered issue workflows and log analysis so the next team could hit the ground running. I also resolved all outstanding production bugs listed in the Release Planner.

projects.name: Inclusive Analytics Platform
projects.description: This platform was built to support more equitable, data-informed decision-making within organizations. I deepened my Chart.js skills here to handle some genuinely complex visualisation needs, and I also took the lead on frontend technical decisions and requirement discussions — which was a valuable experience in balancing stakeholder needs with what's practically achievable.

projects.name: Client Case Study Builder
projects.description: A tool I genuinely enjoyed building — it helps the team present project case studies to clients in a polished, transparent way. I spent time researching RevealJS and got it working smoothly as our presentation engine. I also integrated Microsoft 365 authentication using MSAL, which made the login experience seamless for internal users.

projects.name: Employee CV Management Portal
projects.description: I led a small but capable team of two developers to build a centralised portal for managing and sharing employee CVs internally. Beyond the technical work, I handled client communication and requirement sessions, and presented our progress throughout. I'm happy to say we delivered on time without cutting corners.

pocs.name: React Query POC
pocs.description: A focused exploration of TanStack Query where I dug into its caching model — working through staleTime, gcTime, and deduplication — and then built out some practical patterns like optimistic updates and infinite scroll to see how they behave in a real app context.

pocs.name: Event-Driven Architecture Warehouse
pocs.description: This POC was a deep dive into event-driven thinking on the frontend — I explored event sourcing patterns and built out a Dead Letter Queue with replay support, which gave me a much better appreciation for resilient, observable frontend architectures.

pocs.name: Restaurant Monorepo
pocs.description: A hands-on experiment with Turborepo-based monorepo structure. I modelled a restaurant billing flow across shared packages and explored how workspace configurations and shared dependencies can be managed cleanly at scale.

articles.title: TanStack Query: The Data Fetching Solution You've Been Looking For
How I Moved My App from Nuxt 3 to Nuxt 4
Design Systems Implementation - Atomic Design Methodology

experience.title: Senior Software Engineer
experience.details: Over the course of my time here, I've had the privilege of contributing to more than five production projects spanning Angular, Vue 3, and legacy stacks — alongside three POCs running in parallel. One of the things I'm most proud of is navigating three major framework transitions within six months without ever letting production quality slip. I've also had the chance to give back to the team by running a department-wide SignalR training and putting together an AI Prompt Library (v1.2) with over 25 prompts that colleagues use regularly.

experience.title: Software Engineer Trainee
experience.description: My journey at Simform began with an intensive training programme covering JavaScript, ReactJS, VueJS, and TypeScript. I also got hands-on with Redux, Redux Toolkit, Vuex, Vuetify, and Tailwind CSS during this period. I built several demos using Vue 3 together with Jest and Vue Test Utils, and had a great time participating in the company Hackathon.

education.degree: Bachelor of Engineering in Information Technology

achievements: Successfully transitioned across 3 major frameworks (Angular → Vue → React) within 6 months while maintaining production code quality
Delivered 5+ production projects across enterprise, healthcare, and analytics domains — plus 3 POCs simultaneously
Led CV Builder project team of 3 members
Created AI Prompt Library benefiting entire Vue.js department

angularConsulting.description: Alongside my full-time role at Simform, I've quietly taken on independent Angular consulting work for enterprise engineering teams. Most of this has involved helping teams move away from ageing codebases — AngularJS, React, jQuery — towards modern Angular, as well as advising on component architecture and shoring up performance. Honestly, this work has stretched my Angular skills in ways that a single production project never could, and has given me a grounded understanding of what it takes to meet the scale and compliance demands that Fortune 500 teams deal with every day.

openSource.name: Awesome JavaScript Collection
openSource.description: A curated open-source collection of more than 30 JavaScript projects — built and shared to help others learn and explore different aspects of the language.

openSource.name: GitHub Finder
openSource.description: A handy little app for looking up GitHub profiles and browsing repositories — a project I built to practise API integration and clean UI presentation.

training.title: Department-level SignalR Training
training.description: I had the pleasure of running a department-wide session on integrating SignalR for real-time features. It was a great opportunity to share practical patterns and help colleagues feel more confident working with WebSocket-based communication in their projects.

training.title: AI Prompt Library v1.2
training.description: I put together a library of over 25 carefully crafted, production-ready AI prompts specifically for the Vue.js department. They cover a wide range of everyday needs — code refactoring, debugging, architecture guidance, API integration, security reviews, testing, performance tuning, accessibility improvements, documentation, and app modernisation. It's been really satisfying to see colleagues get genuine value out of it.
